content.full_text:
Fair Campaign Practices Commission
Martin Luther King, Jr.
Civic Center Building
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, California 94704

(415) 644-6380
TTY 644-6915

August 21, 1991

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice Letter

Dear Colleague:

I am writing to you on behalf of the City of Berkeley's Fair Campaign Practices Commission (FCPC). During the November 1990 election and following Judge Karlton's ruling in SEIU v. FPPC, the FCPC received several inquiries from candidates regarding the applicability of Government Code section 85304 to candidates for local municipal elections. At that time, I was verbally advised that because the City of Berkeley had a local ordinance which limited campaign contributions to $250 per election, Government Code section 85304 prohibited local candidates from having multi-candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign contributions to other candidates. The FCPC would like clarification for local candidates whether this interpretation of Section 85304 is still correct and therefore requests that your response be in the form of a written advice letter.

If you need further information, please feel free to contact me.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

SARAH REYNOSO
FCPC Staff Counsel

SR:lh

cc: FCPC Commissioners

sections.question: Whether Government Code section 85304 prohibits local candidates in Berkeley from having multi-candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign contributions to other candidates, given that the City of Berkeley has a local ordinance limiting campaign contributions to $250 per election.
sections.question_synthetic: The Berkeley Fair Campaign Practices Commission seeks clarification on whether Government Code section 85304, in light of Judge Karlton's ruling in SEIU v. FPPC, prohibits local candidates from establishing multi-candidate controlled committees and transferring contributions when the local jurisdiction has a $250 contribution limit.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Berkeley FCPC to the state FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal Question or Conclusion sections present. Document requests written advice from FPPC on interpretation of section 85304 post-SEIU v. FPPC ruling.

embedding.summary: The City of Berkeley's Fair Campaign Practices Commission requests an FPPC advice letter regarding the interpretation of Government Code section 85304 and its application to local candidates following a judicial ruling, specifically concerning whether candidates can have multi-candidate committees and transfer contributions under Berkeley's $250 contribution limit.
